content: Notes to Accompany 6th April 2007 44th Update – Civil Procedure Rules – Justice UK Cookie Settings Are you OK with cookies? We use small files called ‘cookies’ on www.justice.gov.uk. Some are essential to make the site work, some help us to understand how we can improve your experience, and some are set by third parties. You can choose to turn off the non-essential cookies. Which cookies are you happy for us to use? I am OK with cookies Only use essential cookies Choose which cookies we use Close Choose which cookies we use Marketing cookies Off On Google Analytics Off On We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the website so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google Analytics to use or share the data about how you use this site. See our Google Analytics cookies Google Analytics Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years _gid These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 24 hours _gat These help us to manage how we collect analytics when we have lots of visitors on the site at one time 10 minutes Google Analytics Events Off On We use Google Analytics events to measure clicks within the pages and extended usage of the site See our Google Analytics Events cookies Google Analytics Events Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years Essential cookies Always on All users These cookies will always need to be on because they make our site work. See our All users cookies All users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_test_cookie This is used to test whether the browser accepts cookies When you close your browser PHPSESSID This is used to link your device to the information sent to the server from your browser. It is typically used to avoid you having to retype information when moving from one page to another. When you close your browser ccfw-banner-hidden Lets us know you have chosen which cookies are used so we can stop the cookie banner appearing when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-gtm-allowed Lets us know you have accepted certain cookies so we can stop them loading when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-time This is used to store the cookie expiry dates so we know when to ask you again for consent. 1 year ccfw_gtm_id This cookie is used to link the cookie consent banner to Google Tag Manager 1 year Logged in users These cookies apply when users log in. They will always be on because they make our site work. See our Logged in users cookies Logged in users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_[hash] This authenticates you when you log in to the admin area When you close your browser wordpress_logged_in This shows the site that you’re logged in and who you are so you can access the functions you need When you close your browser wordpress_sec If you are logged in as a site admin, this stores your authentication details. When you close your browser wp-settings-{time}-[UID] The number on the end [UID] is your individual user ID from the users database. 1 year > Save cookie preferences . Home   Courts Procedure rules Offenders Search Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter Notes to Accompany 6th April 2007 44th Update Home » Courts » Procedure rules » Civil » contents » frontmatter » Notes to Accompany 6th April 2007 44th Update Menu ≡ Notes to Accompany 6th April 2007 44th Update Coming into force All the amendments included in this update come into force on 6th April 2007, with the following exceptions; 1st January 2007 PD 55B 1st March 2007 PD 54 Introduction The 44 th Update to the Civil Procedure Rules introduces changes in a large number of areas. Part 3 is amended to incorporate the changes made to Part 36 & Part 37 Part 14 is amended to give admissions made before the commencement of proceedings equal weight with those made during proceedings. It will allow parties to give notice in writing whether to admit the truth on any part or whole of the case before proceedings. Parties are also allowed to withdraw a pre-action admission. The new rule 14.1A will not apply to an admission made before 6th April 2007. Part 27 is amended to incorporate the changes made to Part 36 & Part 37. Part 36, Part 37, PD 36, PD 37 are amended to incorporate provisions for removing the requirement for defendants to make an offer to settle money claims in the form of a payment into court. Defendants wishing to settle a claim with a money offer can simply make a written offer to settle. Under the new rule an accepted offer must be paid within 14 days or the claimant will be able to enter judgment and the defendant will lose the costs protection afforded by Part 36. Part 41, Part 44, Part 45, Part 47 and Part 52 are amended to incorporate the changes made to Part 36 & Part 37. PD 7B is amended as a result of the Consumer Credit Act 2006, which amends the Consumer Credit Act 1974. PD 8 is amended to include a number of claims and applications previously dealt with in scheduled rules. A number of scheduled rules have been revoked as a consequence. The opportunity has also been taken to simplify the Practice Direction. PD 23 is amended to extend the pilot scheme for telephone hearings to all county courts & district registries. PD25 is amended to include reference to the fact that there is no longer a privilege against self-incrimination in relation to fraud cases. PD54 is amended to include a new section dealing with applications to judicially review a decision of the Immigration and Nationality Directorate to remove a person from the United Kingdom. PD 55B is amended to make the Possession Claims on Line service (PCOL) easier to use by reducing the amount of detail about arrears which need to be provided at the time a claim is issued. The Pre-Action Protocol for Construction and Engineering Disputes has been revised and reissued following consultation to address issues relating to the time and cost of complying with the protocol. A full list of the changes is set out below. Rules Part 3 Rule 3.1(6A) , text after ‘party in the proceedings’ deleted. Part 14 Rule 14.1 , heading amended. Rule 14.1(5) , new text substituted. Rule 14.1A , new rule inserted after 14.1(5). Part 27 Rule 27.2(1)(g) , words ‘payments into court’ deleted. Rule 27.14(3) , text in brackets deleted. Part 36 New part replaces existing part. Part 37 New part replaces existing part. Part 41 Rule 41.3A , new rule inserted after 41.3. Part 44 Rule 44.3 (4)(c) , text amended. Note in brackets deleted. Rule at 44.12(1) (b) text amended and (c) deleted. Part 45 Rule 45.3(2) , deleted. Part 47 Rule 47.7 , table ‘or a payment into court’ deleted. Part 52 Rules 52.12 (1), (2) and (3) , new text substituted. New text inserted after 52.12. Part 65 New Section VI inserted after rule 65.30. Practice Directions Practice Direction 2B New text substituted at paragraph 5.1. New text substituted at paragraph 5.1(d). New text substituted at paragraph 8.1A. Paragraph 11.1 text deleted. Practice Direction 4 New text add at paragraph 3.2. Title of forms N16(A) and N110A have been substituted. Title of form N242A has been substituted. Form N243A omitted. Practice Direction 5 Paragraph 5.3(9)(b) deleted. Practice Direction 7B New text substituted at paragraph 3.1(5). New sub-para 3.1(5A) inserted. New text substituted at paragraphs 10.1 and 10.2. New paragraphs 10.2A and 10.3 inserted. Practice Direction 8 New Practice Direction replaces existing Practice Direction. Practice Direction 8B Practice Direction deleted. Practice Direction 14 New text substituted at paragraph 1.1. New paragraph 7 inserted. Practice Direction 21 New text in brackets substituted after paragraph 12.3. Practice Direction 23 New text substituted at paragraph 6. Practice Direction 23C Practice Direction deleted. Practice Direction 25 New text substituted at paragraph 7.9(2). Practice Direction 34 New text substituted in Annex C table. Practice Direction 36 New Practice Direction replaces existing Practice Direction. Practice Direction 36B New Practice Direction created. Practice Direction 37 New Practice Direction replaces existing Practice Direction. Practice Direction 41B Paragraph 6, text amended. Practice Direction 43–48 New text substituted at paragraph 8.4. Text deleted at paragraph 40.4(c). Practice Direction 52 Paragraph 8.2 table text amended. Practice Direction 53 Text deleted at paragraph 6.1. Paragraph 6.2, text deleted and substituted. Practice Direction 54 New Section II added. Practice Direction 55B New text inserted at start of paragraph 6.3. New paragraphs 6.3A to C inserted. Practice Direction 65 New Section VI added. Practice Direction 74B Annex substituted. Schedule 1 RSC O.93 Rules 2, 5(2) and (3) and 22 revoked. RSC O.94 Rules 1 to 3, 14 and 15 revoked. RSC O.95 Rules 2 and 3 revoked. RSC O.96 Rules 2 to 8 revoked. RSC O.110 Revoked. Schedule 2 CCR O.45 Rule 1 revoked. CCR O.46 Revoked. CCR O.49 Rules 7 and 12 revoked. Pre-Action Protocols Pre-Action Protocol for Personal Injury First sentence of paragraph 3.9 deleted. Pre-Action Protocol for Construction and Engineering Disputes New protocol substituted for existing protocol. Forms New versions of N16, N16A, N110A and N242A are made available.
